---
title: "Vignettes"
subtitle: "Cross-disciplinary worked examples from the workshop"
---

Vignettes document the analytical approaches developed during the workshop, combining scientific motivation, step-by-step methodology, and fully reproducible R code. Each vignette covers a specific SA–IDM integration example applied to the case study.

::: {.callout-note}
Vignettes will be added here as they are finalized during and after the workshop.
:::

## Group A — Key Epidemiological Parameters

*Vignettes to be added*

## Group B — Serial Interval Distribution

*Vignettes to be added*

## Group C — Reporting Delays and Underreporting

*Vignettes to be added*

## Group D — Vaccine Effectiveness

*Vignettes to be added*

---

## Contributing a Vignette

Vignettes are Quarto (`.qmd`) files committed to the `vignettes/` folder on your group's branch. See the [Group Analysis Scripts](../analysis/index.qmd) page for first-time setup and how to work with branches.

### Create your vignette file

Add a new `.qmd` file to the `vignettes/` folder using the naming convention `vignette-groupX-topic.qmd`, e.g.:

```
vignettes/vignette-groupA-reproduction-number.qmd
```

### Vignette template

````markdown
---
title: "Your Vignette Title"
author: "Author Names"
date: today
---

## Scientific Background

## Methods

## Data

## Analysis

## Results

## Discussion and Limitations

## Session Info

```{r}
sessionInfo()
```
````

### Push your vignette

```bash
git add vignettes/vignette-groupA-topic.qmd
git commit -m "Add Group A vignette: reproduction number estimation"
git push origin group-A
```

The organizers will merge group branches into `main` so vignettes appear on the site.
